name: Martinique (France)

colors.symbolism: the blue field of the four quarters
the cross of dividing lines and the four serpents

description: A blue field divided by a white cross into four equal quarters, each charged with a white L-shaped serpent (the fer-de-lance pit viper native to the island).
history: Originated from a French royal ordinance of 4 August 1766 establishing a flag for ships of Martinique and Saint Lucia. Used historically as a colonial naval flag during the period of French slavery in the Caribbean, the flag's use has become highly controversial because of its colonial-era associations. In 2018 the Collectivité Territoriale de Martinique discontinued official use of the snake flag, and in 2023 a new official flag (red, green and black tricolour with a hummingbird) was adopted. The snake flag is therefore historic / no longer in official use, though it remains the most widely recognised image associated with Martinique outside the island.

features.symbolism: a blue field divided into four quarters by a white cross, derived from a 1766 royal ordinance
the white cross dividing the field into four quarters, derived from a 1766 royal ordinance for ships of Martinique and Saint Lucia
four white L-shaped serpents (fer-de-lance, the venomous pit viper native to the island), one in each quarter
